entry.title: CAP Multitenancy with Objectstore
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community

I would like to use a CAP application in Java for multitenancy. Apart from the HANA DB HDI container creation feature, I would also like to create objectstore instances for each tenant upon subscription.

Is there a way to achieve this by "intercepting" the request when the subscribe endpoint is called?

/-/cds/deployment/subscribe

Also, am I able to achieve this using CAP without having to implement my own Service Manager utility class? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Release of SAP Disclosure Management Stack 2200
entry.content:
Thank you for the update - only one question:

By when will help.sap.com display the stack 22xx - currently there is no newer version than 21xx available.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DISCLOSURE_MANAGEMENT?locale=en-US 

best regards, Martin

entry.title: Re: Resolving the "Enqueue Operation Without Progress" Error in SAP & Avoiding SPOF
entry.content: Helped.Thanks

entry.title: Re: No visible border for grid table in MDK
entry.content: @Sourabh1  As Bill already mentioned, there is no column separator option in the Grid Table. If this is a feature you need for your mobile app, please submit a feature request via this link https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2279, and we will be happy to review and prioritize it accordingly.

entry.title: Re: Change a dataset in a SAC story and to keep the story...
entry.content: When you create model from model, the default one is with 'new model type'. For how to replace, please read through the blog.

entry.title: Re: Sign the XML content with PKCS7 signer in CPI
entry.content:
Hello Nisha,
Hope you are doing well..!

I Have similar requirement where i need to sign the <Body> node and its child using key store certificate, convert to base64 and then place it under singautreValue tag like below before GZip.

could share me how you achieve your requirement so that i can simulate it with mine 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<Message>

  <Header>

    <Sender>TEST</Sender>

    <Receiver>TEST</Receiver>

    <MessageType>TEST</MessageType>

    <MessageDescription>TEST</MessageDescription>

    <TimeStamp>2024-11-19T11:27:31</TimeStamp>

  </Header>

  <Body>

    <PayrollMessage>

      <PayrollMessageRef>TEST</PayrollMessageRef>

      <PayrollMessageType>TEST</PayrollMessageType>

      <PayrollTransactionCount>1</PayrollTransactionCount>

      <PayrollTransctionAmount>00000.00</PayrollTransctionAmount>

      <PayrollTransaction>

        <SequenceNum>00000</SequenceNum>

        <TransactionData>:20:43733

          :32A:241119XXX0000

          :50:TEST

          :52A:TEST

          :53B:/0000000000

          :57A:TEST

          :59:/00000000000000000000

          TEST

           CITY

          :70:TEST

          :72:000000

          00000,00

          0000,00

          00000,00

          0,00</TransactionData>

        </PayrollTransaction>

      </PayrollMessage>

    </Body>

    <Signature>

      <SignatureValue>MIAGCSqGSIb3DQEHAqCAMIACAQExDzANBglghkgBZQMEAgEFADCABgkqhkiG9w0B==</SignatureValue>

    </Signature>

  </Message>

entry.title: Re: http status 404 not found when login SLD
entry.content: the SAP note is not relevant, we've found out the issue due to the b1c_extAddress.conf in the proxy server mapped to wrong folder, we will need to change the root controlcenter folder to match the nginx installation folder. issue is resolved.

entry.title: since SAP Update it´s not possible to export data as a text file with tcode IW37n
entry.content: since SAP Update it´s not possible to export data as a text file with tcode IW37Nbefore SAP updateafter SAP update

entry.title: Re: How to perform looping in SAP PaPM cloud based on a c...
entry.content:
Hi Hanna,

We have a community blogpost that might help your concern above by taking advantage of the "Advanced Tab" part of the View/Iterative View Function. You may refer to the blogpost below:

View Function: Advanced Tab

As an additional information on View function, you may refer here: SAP PaPM: Application Help: View

To know more about SAP Profitability and Performance Management - Standard Model, please visit our Application Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_PROFITABILITY_PERFORMANCE_MANAGEMENT_CLOUD/56471df1959f4cfd9e3bf7a6d2d5be42/56a0a0a1d8b10f63e10000000a42189c.html

Thanks and regards,

Gene

entry.title: Re: SAP API Management - OAuth 2.0 authentication with Su...
entry.content:
Hi Faisal, 

Keeping my answers short. 

You are right that the policy template my colleague referred to in the blog is now deprecated. Please stay tuned, I'm publishing an updated policy template soon. The new approach will use SAML Assertions generated and signed by API management as documented here. 

Meanwhile, pls note that today we've published a new template that uses SAMBearerAssertion approach directly by using Microsoft EntraID as the identity provider if SuccessFactors is configured so. A blog for this approach will be out soon too. 

Coming to your questions :

1. API Management uses TLSv1.3 (if client can negotiate), else fallback is TLSv1.2. You can easily verify this in a tool like curl.

2. The current approach does not act as an IDP. If you closely look at the sequence, it just invokes the /oauth/idp endpoint and directly gets the SAMLToken from SFSF.

entry.title: Re: Billing status uodate in outbound delivery
entry.content: Hi, please check your copy control settings in transaction VTFL

entry.title: How to set different watermarks for D/T/P system?
entry.content:
Hi SAP，

I used the master template. The print template is modified from D and transferred to P. I only open the form template using ALD and see the watermark field. If I adjust the watermark value in ALD, this will also be transferred to the P system. But I don't want to be the same as the watermark in D system. How do I set this?

my system is S4HANA Cloud public edition.

Thanks,

Lingling

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements preview not displaying cds view fields
entry.content:
I guess you need to annotate the fields of the CDS view to display them in the app.

This can be done with: 

 

@ui.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]

entry.title: Applications, Single and Composite roles are not displayed in IAG Access request
entry.content:
Hi All,

While trying to raise a Access request in IAG, no single role, composite role is appearing to be selected. Also, if a user has a application and want to add more roles for the same application, then the same does  not appear . 

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Is RFC connection used in SAP Public Cloud ?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Is RFC still used in SAP Public Cloud ? When i’m trying to access AFO using SAP public Cloud i received below screenshot error. And after adding Business Catalog: SAP_BW_BC_AOF_PC the issue was resolved 

But wanted to understand the reason behind the error message ?

entry.title: Re: Revenue Recognition(Service Order with WBS assigned)
entry.content:
Hi Soma,

in case of service orders, the revenue recognition for Service Documents will apply, as the WBS element account assignment in service orders are only statistical. Please refer to my blog on the topic for further details.

Warm regards
Varun

entry.title: On-stack Clean Core Extensibility: Plain and Simple
entry.content:
One of the things our team has been told is that "We have to decide for ourselves what is clean and what is not". This has proven to be very unhelpful and not factual.

The truth is: there is no mystery as to what constitutes clean extensibility. SAP is actually surprisingly clear on this topic.

Consider the following diagram, which you've probably seen before:

Here we are considering primarily the on-stack section.

What are the tiers?

The definition of Tier 1 is all your developments that use only released-for-cloud objects, which makes those objects clean and therefore upgrade-safe.

(Objects that are released for cloud are guaranteed by SAP to have a stable interface, hence anything that uses them is guaranteed not to break in an upgrade).

Tier 2 is just a way for you to mitigate the cases where SAP may not have provided a released object, so you create a (temporary) wrapper as a workaround.

So, for example, creating a table that only refers to released data elements, is tier 1.

Creating a table that uses data elements that are not released would be tier 2. (Which is a bad example, because you would in that case probably just create the data elements yourself).

Just creating a table or other object does not suggest Tier 1 or Tier 2; it's whether the object uses released objects or not that decides in which tier it is.

A better example is if you create a CDS view: There, it is more likely that you might have to rely on some view of SAP that is not released. In that case, the recommendation is to create a wrapper around the unreleased object, mark the wrapper as released for cloud, and use that in your CDS view. That way, your view remains  clean (Tier 1), but the wrapper is in Tier 2.

Then, if and when SAP releases something you can use to replace your tier 2 object, you retire your wrapper  and swap it out with the released object.

Using this approach, you reduce the risk that you are creating objects that might cause issues with upgrades by making it easier to keep track of the state of "cleanness" of your system.

Tier 3 covers everything that SAP says you must not use, like Dynpro, WebDynpro, unreleased BAdIs, etc. But this is anyway more applicable to people who are migrating developments from an old system, such as in a brownfield scenario. In a greenfield scenario, by contrast, you will most likely avoid creating such developments to begin with. In a brownfield scenario, the idea is to progressively retire all your Tier 3 developments and replace them with Tier 1.

In the case of side-by-side extensibility, where you operate outside of the S/4 system or "off-stack", you can only consume released APIs anyway, which means there is no risk of breaking something in an upgrade, so that is clean by default.

What a released-for-cloud object is

To be clear, this is what we are talking about:

This is what you see when you go to the API State properties of an object that is released for cloud development.

When you make use of the "ABAP for Cloud Development" language version (a.k.a. "ABAP Cloud"), the compiler checks whether the objects you use have been released for cloud and will prevent you from compiling otherwise.

This prevents you from accidentally incorporating non-released objects in your code.

You can tell if an object uses this version of ABAP in the properties of the object:

If you set this language version on a package, objects created in that package will by default also use it, but you can override this per object to use the "Standard ABAP" language version, which would be the case for a wrapper object.

How this ties in with AEM (SAP Application Extension Methodology)

In short, it doesn't, really.

This is another rabbit hole we've been led down, believing that AEM is somehow supposed to help us identify what is clean core. But that is not its purpose.

AEM is a methodology that helps formulate an approach to deciding what technologies to use to fulfil a requirement. But the assumption is that you already know what is clean and what is not, and that is what I try to convey in simple terms above.

entry.title: Re: Missing F110 Payments in BNK_MONI
entry.content:
Hi,

After running the payment program F110, you need to run the transaction FBPM1 to merge the payments and generate the batch. Then, run the transaction BNK_MONIP to check if the batch has been properly created.

The standard process:
1. Transaction BNK_MONIP to check whether a merge has occurred. Red traffic light: The merge has occurred, but the system did not create a batch.Then, use transaction SLG1 and the object 'FBPM' to check the application log and use transaction SM37 to check the jobs with the identifier PAY_BTC*.

2.Solved the problem identified on the job log  and then, you can use the report RBNK_PAYM_SCHEDULE to trigger the batch creation again.

Check also the SAP FAQ note: 1577912 and KBA 2775848 - BCM: Batch is not created.

Hope this help

Best regards,

Mahira

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps for SAP Business One, the next-gen Development Environment

According to exercises documents, I am created a new page to load Purchase Order from SAP but the Page Parameter (don't have Number type) so I can not assign value to DocEntry to load the details data.

Thank you so much.

With Best Regards,

Tam

entry.title: remote enabled function module to read material configuration on plant level
entry.content:
Hi,

I am looking for a "remote enabled function module to read material configuration" on plant level

to read this configuration with our "Data Service" server, like in SAP over Tx MD04.

I found one ( CUCB_GET_VALUES_FROM_INST_RFC ), but this produce an error in our ERP system.

Or is there a way, to read the configuration out from tables?

Thx

Holger

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign - State Wise GST Configuration
entry.content:
• Dear @prathibha_b  and Team,

                        We are from Tamilnadu in India , with our registered head office in Tamil Nadu. We have more than 20 client projects in 15 different states, including Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, Maharasta, and Delhi. We have multiple GST number based on state-wise ,and we maintain state-wise GST numbers for our accounts receivable and accounts payable processes. This is our business scenario.

How to capture this business scenario in SAP Business ByDesign in from the Organization chart level: 

As per our requirement:

Head Office (Tamil Nadu): We use the Tamil Nadu GST number for accounts receivable and accounts payable.
Customer: For project-related transactions.
Supplier: For material-related transactions.
Service: For service-related transactions, such as vehicle rent and room rent

Branch Office & Multiple Project Locations:
Kerala: We use the Kerala GST number for accounts receivable and accounts payable.
Customer: For project-related transactions.
Supplier: For material-related transactions.
Service: For service-related transactions, such as vehicle rent and room rent

Andhra: We use the Andhra GST number for accounts receivable and accounts payable.
Customer: For project-related transactions.
Supplier: For material-related transactions.
Service: For service-related transactions, such as vehicle rent and room rent

Maharashtra: We use the Maharashtra GST number for accounts receivable and accounts payable.
Customer: For project-related transactions.
Supplier: For material-related transactions.
Service: For service-related transactions, such as vehicle rent and room rent

we are loking for your valuble support 

Thanks & Regards 

C.Vijaykannan

9345922099

vijaykannan@live.com

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Maintenance Windows and Major Upgrade Windows in 2024

Hello,

the dates for 2411 have already been updated and can be seen soon. 

Regards, Andreas

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements preview not displaying cds view fields
entry.content: Hi @MBartsch71 , I have already tried this but have not noticed a change in the fields displayed

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Set Up Rules for Who Can Create and Transport SAP Build Projects
entry.content:
Not sure exactly what you are asking but I will try to summarize as best I can:

  • You would have 3 BTP subaccounts, each with SAP Build
  • You would set up CTMS for those 3 tenants
  • You would use transport to move apps/processes from dev to test to prod
  • On each tenant you would still have to build and deploy, as best I can understand. The transport manages the design artifacts, again as best as I understand

2 notes:

  • From what I can see on my system, there currently is no transport for VCF/Apps backend -- I could not find a way to create versioning or do transport. Perhaps this is on the roadmap.
  • This session was about setting up rules for who can create and transport projects, but was not about CTMS or how to do transport.

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh - limit of 32767 reached

entry.title: India Business: Keeping Backup of books of Account Leveraging SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
Enhanced Record-Keeping Regulations for Indian Businesses

As of August 5, 2022, Indian businesses must adhere to new record-keeping regulations introduced by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA). These regulations, outlined in the Companies (Accounts) Fourth Amendment Rules, 2022, mandate that companies ensure their electronic records are accessible within India at all times. Additionally, businesses are required to maintain a daily backup of their books of account data within the country.

Key Requirements

  • Accessibility: Electronic records must be accessible within India at all times.
  • Daily Backup: A daily backup of books of account data must be stored within India.

Leveraging SAP S/4HANA for Compliance

For businesses using SAP S/4HANA, compliance can be streamlined by utilizing the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) statutory reporting solution. This tool helps users extract the necessary books of account data from their SAP S/4HANA systems, ensuring they meet the new regulatory requirements.

Guidance and Support

For detailed guidance on how to implement these changes, SAP has provided SAP Note 3443920 .

Important Note: Please be aware that the information provided here is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal or regulatory advice. Businesses should consult with their legal advisors to ensure full compliance with the new regulations.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, feel free to reach out. We're here to help you navigate these new requirements smoothly.

Karthiheyini Subbiah

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi @Dan_Wroblewski,

I assigned these roles to Default Identity provider.

And the inbox is got because the other user that I used in Recipient User also subscribed "SAP Build Process Automation" service.

This inbox is SAP Build's Inbox. Open form the yellow highlighted icon.

entry.title: Re: WBS Element to Control Budgeting(Capex)

entry.title: Outbound Delivery show error msg: VL063
entry.content:
Hi All,

Please help, 

The current outstanding sales quantity is 195,000, and an over-delivery tolerance of 10% has been set.

However, the delivery quantity in DN can only be assigned a maximum of 142,000. and setting the quantity to 142,001 will result in a error message VL063-Overdelivery tolerance of 10% has been exceeded.

Can anyone help?

Thanks All !!

entry.title: c4c v2 - How do we get the historical changes done to Opportunity in REST service?
entry.content:
Hello community,

We are looking for historical changes made to the Opportunity from the REST service available Overview | Opportunity Service | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

We had the entity to retrieve the historical changes in v1. Refer to this OData SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Can someone please help to understand how to retrieve the history in REST service? I came to know that the REST service has the attribute called "snapshots", but it never returns when the request is made.

Regards,
Pratik. 

C4C Sales C4C Service C4C Analytics

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi @Dan_Wroblewski,

Normally, the work of admin is

1. they can add new users in the system

2. they can decide which user can access which part of the system

3. they can add access to other user as new admin, etc...

 

So, I would like to know the admin role of user in SAP Build Process Automation service is same like other?

And as I checked,

1. I created one approval workflow and I assigned myself as Admin role by assigning "Administrator" role in BTP account.

2. I can access the approval form workflow and can also able to access Inbox that can decide "Approve" or "Reject" the approval form.

3. I added new users in Cloud Identity Service and these users are also able to access the form link directly by using their own user and password.

4. I assigned the ProcessAutomationAdmin, ProcessAutomationParticipant roles to the new user accounts from my BTP account.

5. But, they cannot access the Inbox of SAP Build automatically although I assigned the required roles to these user accounts.

6. I needed to login the other new user BTP account and need to assign the required roles.

7. After then, that new user account also access the Inbox of SAP Build. 

8. I updated the Recipient User of Workflow as the new user mail. But the approval request of "Approve" or "Reject" is not shown in Inbox of new user.

 

I checked the documentation about user assignment provided by SAP and it is hard to understood for me, how the User Role assignment and Admin role works in SAP Build Process Automation.

So, I am checking that I miss some steps (or) do something wrong by asking questions in community blog.

Could you please point out for me if I understood wrongly?

entry.title: Externalized Parameters are not displayed in the configuration view
entry.content:
Hey Community,

I would like to create parameters that can be configured. The background is as follows. When I deploy an integration flow from the test system to the production system, I no longer want to have to make changes within the iFlow. It should be sufficient to adjust the configurable parameters.

In this test scenario, I have created the two externalized parameters "Test1" & "Test2".

My expectation would now be that I have the possibility to set appropriate values for these parameters via the "configure" action of the iFlow. However, in the configure view, for some inexplicable reason, only the last added parameter is available to me.

Does anyone have an idea why this could be? I have already tested the classics such as reloading the page or clearing the brwoser cache and these can be ruled out.

I am grateful for any advice.
Greetings Marc

entry.title: Re: Extend Datasphere Business content package.
entry.content: I also tried to look in the documentation but couldn't find anything. I would like to follow this thread if anyone or SAP has a suggestion.

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP...
entry.content: 2 things I'm confused about: How did the other user log into SAP Build (meaning which IDP)? When you say the other user "subscribed" to process automation, hat is confusing, because "subscribing to a service" is done by the admin when they essentially install the service on the subaccount, so what does it the user "subscribed ... o the service"?

entry.title: Re: Outbound Delivery show error msg: VL063
entry.content:
Hello @0819 

It's standard behavior based on the configuration.

Could you provide more details on what you want to achieve?

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Bulk Update in CAP (Node.js)
entry.content:
Just as a remark: This slows down the async process way too much, as you wait for each Promise to resolve before updating the next one.

Instead of:

 

        for (let upd of arrUpdate) {
            await db.run(UPDATE(Rates).with(upd))
        }

 

 Do:

 

let promises = []
for (let upd of arrUpdate) {
    promises.push(db.run(UPDATE(Rates).with(upd)))
}
await Promise.all(promises)

entry.title: API to read data from Manage Workforce app
entry.content: I would like to know if there is API to read data from Manage Workforce app in S4HANA system.

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements preview not displaying cds view fields
entry.content: After some testing, I found that activating the view via the SAP GUI as well as the @EndUserText.Label annotation seemed to help with the field names.

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi @Dan_Wroblewski,

We have 2 emails like user1@company.com and user2@company.com.

And we created BTP account for both email.

So, user1@company.com subscribed to BTP account and has its own subaccount.

And also, user2@company.com subscribed to BTP account and has its own subaccount too.

 

The workflow is created by user1@company.com and we would like to set many approvers in our workflow.

eg. if amount > 100 - approval of user1@company.com will be needed.

      if amount > 200 - approval of both user1@company.com and user2@company.com will be needed.

So, we want to give Inbox and Permission of "Approve" or "Reject" access to both users.

 

And these access are ok for user1@company.com who is created the workflow. But for the user2@company.com, we don't unsure how to give these access.

entry.title: Re: Extend Validity of E-Waybill
entry.content:
You can refer to the Electronic Waybill guide on the SAP help portal for complete implementation and configuration of the solution.

For extending electronic waybill validity, see Extend Electronic Waybill Validity.

We recommend you to implement the new development (See Electronic Waybills) that automatically provides the file to submit based on the submitted electronic documents as compared to the old development (See Electronic Waybills (Legacy)) that requires manual efforts and has a reduced scope.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 and Concur-Integration
entry.content:
Dear Afzal,

please DON'T deinstall the CTE_* add-ons. 
An upgrade from SAP ECC to S/4HANA 2020 upwards is supported and the add-ons are automatically transformed into the S/4HANA Core Source Code. 
If you would uninstall the add-ons you would loose all your data, which could lead to business issues. 

The corresponding note for the SAP Concur Integration is https://me.sap.com/notes/3233748.
The following info is contained in there:
Starting with release S/4HANA 2020 your system will automatically receive the newest software version, since the integration functionality will be delivered as a core functionality and not as add-on anymore.
There are no additional steps required before or after upgrade when upgrading from the add-ons to the releases S/4HANA 2020 and higher. 
Best regards,
Mathias

entry.title: SAP Code-Jam Request in Dubai
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Greetings of the day!

We would like to kindly request a SAP Code Jam event to be hosted at the Bosch office in Dubai. We anticipate an audience of approximately 30-35 participants and would like to extend an open invitation to all interested individuals.

The topic we are particularly interested in is:
With SAP Build: Create Apps, Processes, and Business Sites Without Coding.

We are aiming to organize this event in December 2024, with the specific date to be decided based on the mutual availability of your team and ours.

We would greatly appreciate your consideration and look forward to hearing from you at your earliest convenience.

--
Warm Regards,
Harish Vatsa

entry.title: Re: Time Sheet Draft and submit
entry.content:
You can also refer to below KBA-

2901734 - How to use "save as draft" functionality in My Timesheet Version 3

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2901734

entry.title: discounted purchase invoice
entry.content:
Hi to all,

When purchasing from a supplier, due to meeting the quantity or value of their promotional program, we receive a discount on the purchase price. The supplier has issued a separate discount invoice. This discount amount is based on numerous POs and invoices. How can I account for this invoice in the MM module and ensure that developed reports on the purchase contract can reflect information about this discount invoice?

Please anyone suggest me.

Regards,

entry.title: Production Order Stored Procedure Blocking Issue for Production
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
I'm using following Stored procedure to block updating Planned qty in production order 
but this stored procedure is blocking users from entering Issue for Production that includes machine or labour

 
IF @object_type='202' AND @transaction_type = 'U'
 
Begin
 
IF EXISTS (SELECT T0.DocEntry
FROM OWOR T0 
inner join WOR1 T1 on t0.docentry = t1.docentry
inner join AWOR T2 on t2.DocEntry =t0.DocEntry 
inner join AWO1 T3 on t3.LogInstanc = T2.LogInstanc and t3.DocEntry=t2.DocEntry and t1.LineNum =t3.LineNum and t1.ItemCode = t3.ItemCode
WHERE T0.DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del 
and T2.status ='R'
and T0.Status = 'R'
and T0.ItemCode like 'F%'
and t0.UserSign2 != '1'
and t3.LogInstanc = (Select MAX(AWO1.loginstanc) from AWO1 where AWO1.docentry =@list_of_cols_val_tab_del )
AND T0.CreateDate > '20241111'
GROUP BY T0.DocEntry
   HAVING SUM(T1.PlannedQty) <> SUM(T3.PlannedQty)
)
 
BEGIN
 
SET @error = 2023021501
SET @error_message = 'Not authorized to Update Planned Qty in  Released Production Order'
 
END
 
END

entry.title: Re: Read Rate plan (with pricing scheme) data / charges v...
entry.content:
Dear Elena, 

introduction of Price Calculation for price determination, rate plans do not contain the prices anymore. That's why in this scenario the rate plan view for example cannot return a list of charges with prices as before. Instead, the role of a rate plan has inherently changed to a link between product and rate plan template.
In the latter, the charges are defined which you require in your request.

In order to fetch the charges, you can first read the rate plan to get the associated rate plan template, and subsequently read the rate plan template to get the list of charges with their rate elements.

Best
Carsten

entry.title: Default TLS 1.2 in SAP API Management - How to check?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

As per the below SAP post, the TLSv 1.2 has automatically been enforced in SAP API Management. So, my understanding is , any request from SAP API Management to backend system would be TLSv 1.2 secured protocol. But I would like to know, from consumer of API perspective, is it also the the same (request from consumer of the API to the SAP API Management) that the request is secured with TLSv 1.2 protocol?. Is there a way to check the TLS version in SAP API Management?. Much appreciate for any response!.

Limits in API Management 

Thanks,

Azeem

entry.title: Re: Outbound Delivery show error msg: VL063
entry.content:
Hi @Vivek_Savant

The problem is that there is still 195,000 outstanding.

If only 142,000 can be delivered, an error will be reported.

Doesn’t that mean there are still 53,000 outstanding?

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Usage-based subscription with minimum fixed consumption per billing cycle
entry.content:
Hi Elena,

to me this sounds either as a scenario as described in the section on the suitable quantity operator:
https://help.sap.com/docs/PRICE_CALCULATION/53987d38467243e28cbeb8514d015666/18135c915fda4a78b5325e3957a7e193.html?locale=en-US
or 
that you can use a Volume price condition with two tiers:
up to 50: fixed price
beyond: linear price

Did you try one of these approaches?
Best regards,
Carsten

entry.title: Re: Regarding the Schedule End Time in Schedule Delivery...
entry.content:
Hello @Karti 

If you want to specify a time range for creating delivery documents for sales orders or purchase orders, do not select the "Start Immediately" checkbox.

Instead, click on Define Recurrence Pattern and set the time range according to your business requirements, as shown in the screenshot below

Best Regars

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Enhancement in IW42
entry.content:
No guarantee - but usually the code line between ECC and S/4HANA Private cloud concerning such SAPGUI transaction is as far in sync that existing official extension points are available also in S4CORE.
In S4core additional/new checks might kick in.

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate road map viewer Now Accessible via SAP for Me
entry.content: Hello! Seems like the link for the roadmap in SAP for me is still the old one, meaning it says there is a new address now and only then redirects me to the roadmap in SAP for me, even when I go via SAP for me itself.

entry.title: Re: Outbound Delivery show error msg: VL063
entry.content:
Hi @Vivek_Savant,

In addition, the total quantity of the original sales order item is 420,000,

which is split into two schedule details with a quantity of 210,000 each.

10% over-delivery should be enough to deliver 462,000.

The first delivery was 225,000 ,so I think this time we should be able to deliver up to 237,000.

If there are any problems with my understanding, please point them out, thank you.

Best Regards

entry.title: Maintenance License can be renew for life long?
entry.content:
Can we apply for a lifetime maintenance license?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: CIO Guide: Solutions to the growing hidden cost of Simplification in the Digital Transformation
entry.content:
In the past a Person might have used 3 or 4 Applications to do their Job, they might have taken Data from one application, copied it to Excel, manipulated it, and then put the Data  "in to SAP" for the final part of a Business Process, for example Accounts Receivable.

With the Digital Transformation, with the Intelligent Enterprise, and Simplification, the goal is that the 3 or 4 Applications are being replaced by 1 Application, 1 Front-End, 1 User-Experience, where People can do their Job altogether in the same place the same Application.

The hidden cost of this, which all SAP Technical Architects, SAP Integration Architects, SAP Security Architects, and SAP Data Architects will be very familiar with, is as we make the Front-End simpler, we make the Back-End exponentially more complicated.

This guide will introduce some options for simplifying the Back-End.

In the not too distant past, many people in your Company would use several Applications to do perform their Role, especially for example in Accounts, like this:

In the journey that we are all on the Simplify, as part of the Digital Transformation and the Intelligent Enterprise, one of the goals is going towards one "Application", one point of entry, where people can perform their Role from one place, a big part of that for many SAP Customers is S/4HANA, and the SAP BTP Work Zone.

This transformation, from many Applications to 1 Application can look something like this, as @Vera_Gutbrod shows in her excellent blog:

 

 

When the End User Experience is simplified for Business Processes which cross over multiple Applications and on top of that, Applications from different Vendors, the hidden price of this Front-End simplification is Back-End complexity.

What I am seeing, is that as the Front-End becomes more simple the Back-End complexity is going up exponentially:

 

Why is this happening, why is simplifying the Front-End, the End-User Experience making the Back-End so complicated ?

There are several dimensions driving the complexity, and maybe you can think of more, to make the Front-End simpler we need to:

. Integrate Applications

. Move Data

. Secure Connectivity & Access & Authorisation across multiple Applications and layers and the Data

. We need to do Mapping and Transformations of Data, either at the SAP End or the other End - this is another challenge to the "Clean-Core" and the S/4 Transformations, there needs to be a principle, mappings/transformation must happen outside of the S/4

. Compliance Requirements

Anybody involved in this kind of work will know how much of their time is being taken these days in Integration and Security Architecture discussions to enable Integrations.

From my experience, this is a lot of work, by a lot of people and a lot of effort, and this is the hidden price of End-User & Front-End Simplification, because, nobody sees all of this effort, apart from the people involved.

So how can we reduce these hidden costs ?

We need to look at the latest technologies and methods, here are some starting points:

. Event based Integrations with SAP Event Mesh and SAP Advanced Event Mesh - publish the data and let the subscribing applications react accordingly

. API Integrations with SAP Integration Suite, Cloud Integration, SAP APIM

. Simplified Security with Networking 2.0  - look at atsign dot com and noports dot com ,  look at how they are simplifying Security with zero attack service and zero trust planes and self sovereign data

. Enterprise Blockchain as a shared common single source of truth for Data within your Organisation and across Organisations

Yes this is more technology, once it is set up, and the Integration Patterns and Principles are in place it will be a case of "rinse and repeat".

What do you think ? How can we reduce the hidden costs of Simplification ?

Andy Silvey.

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 25 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 10 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

Andy is also co-Founder of atkrypto  inc, an startup whose ambition is to make Blockchain easy for Enterprise.

atkrypto dot io's flagship product is the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP,  and atkrypto.io is a SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner. 

The atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has been designed by SAP Independent Experts for the needs of SAP Customers and to be deployed on the SAP BTP Kyma Runtime Service and leverage native integration to SAP Products.

atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has a number of unique qualities, including being the only Blockchain software in the world which has a DataCenter version and a light mobile version which can run on Edge/IoT/Mobile devices and enables data to be written to the Blockchain at the Edge where that same Blockchain is running on a Server in the DataCenter, protecting the integrity and originality of data from the Edge to Insights. Taking Blockchain to the Data at the Edge instead of taking the Data to the Blockchain.

All of this makes atkrypto dot io the DePIN Decentralised Physical Infrastructure Network solution for Enterprise.

entry.title: Re: Maintenance License can be renew for life long?
entry.content:
Hi Mohd,

According to 1898812 - How to renew a maintenance certificate and/or remove duplicates - SAP for Me, a maintenance certificate has a validity of three months (90 days) .

Unfortunately, there is no ability to make it permanent. However, there is an option to renew maintenance certificates using Automatic Updates (Solution Manager). Using SAP Solution Manager's automatic update feature to generate updated maintenance certificates every 90 days is recommended. When Automatic Distribution is set up correctly, Solution Manager automatically renews the maintenance certificate(s) so there is no need to manually update them in the License Keys app. For more information, visit the Maintenance Certificates online help.

To configure License Management for automatic updates of Maintenance certificates, follow the steps outlined in this document: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/c413647f87a54db59d18cb074ce3dafd/4142b6397c604278b6626f3d570c98c8.html?q=maintenance%20certificate

If you have any questions regarding this matter, please create a case for the Solution Manager Landscape Management team under component SV-SMG-LDB.

Hope the above information is helpful to you.

Best regards,
Judy

entry.title: Re: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD EDITION - CDS_VIEW FOR TAX RATE I...
entry.content: Thank you Jerry. Unfortunately these apps do not show data. Seems I_CsgnmtAndSTOTxPrpslTaxItem requires consignment or stock transfer orders and I_PurOrdPricingElementAPI01 retrieves the pricing element fields of a purchase order. Nevermind, I am strongly confused about it. How can we search cds view based on this field KBETR?? Such as tricky way to find something that other SAP versions was quite easy 😞 BR! Guillermo

entry.title: Re: How to create custom analytical profit & loss query for current vs previous year
entry.content: Thanks very much for your question; it has been forwarded to the experts.

entry.title: Re: Opening Balances: Group Financial Statements - Review...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry that it hasn't been answered until now.
If the issue still persist, may I kindly ask you to create an incident?
The product experts would like to take a closer look.

Please accept my answer to close this question. Thanks very much.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: European Sustainability Reporting Standards(ESRS) Journey With SAP Sustainability Control Tower(
entry.content:
Hi, @Pardhasaradhi_Reddy_Chelikam , great post! I have a few questions:

1) This question is about SCT data collection in general, not specifically to ESRS. I have read that for the measure amount you can import data from S/4 HANA. However, you can only do it once, and then the task would be marked as complete, right?  How would you handle an scenario with multiple ERPs, for example, for a complex multinational? Is there a way to import from multiple ERPs?

2) About ESRS, do the metrics include the ESRS EFRAG Code? Is it possible to include it in a report?

3) You included the following comment in your blog "Please bear in mind that the transition of ESRS journey with SAP SCT is underway".  What does this mean exactly and when would the transition be completed?

4) Are you familiar with the ESRS Disclosure Management Content from PAPM (ESRS Disclosure Management | SAP Help Portal)?. Have you explored it?

Kind regards, Leticia

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 and Concur-Integration
entry.content:
Dear Mathias,

excellent. Thank you very much for your quick response.

I had some clue and also had this SAP Note, but still was confused about the procedure.

So, thanks again!

Best Regards,

Afzal

entry.title: Re: c4c v2 - How do we get the historical changes done to...
entry.content:
Hi Kunal,

Thanks for answering.

I have tried the lifeCycleStatus entity within the OpportunityService. 

Its just the master list of available lifeCycleStatus' applicable for any Opportunity. It does not represent the history of any Opportunity.

Thanks,
Pratik

entry.title: Re: On-stack Clean Core Extensibility: Plain and Simple
entry.content:
It's a good blog.

I agree that AEM is an approach that deciding which technologies to use to meet requirements. In other words, it means that it is not easy to develop only with the Clean Core way. I'm not sure which one is easier that an old system consisting only of Tier 3 vs. running a system mixed with Tiers 1 to 3. 

I think this is why Clean Core is difficult in Brown Field. I expect SAP to provide migration guide for Tier 3.

entry.title: Emissions data for the services used under RISE with SAP
entry.content:
Our customer is using S/4HANA Private Edition and SAP Analytics Cloud. They need to report the emissions data across the IT landscape, including the ones under RISE with SAP. 

How are the RISE customers getting the emission figures and the values allocated for their usage? Any dashboard within SAP for Me?

regards

Anil

entry.title: Re: Regarding "Data Overview" of SAP for Me
entry.content: 質問があります。 https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/dataoverview で、2024年10月はシステム「SID_100」の結果が、2024年11月はシステム「SID_000」の結果がそれぞれ表示されています。 10月と11月でクライアントが異なるにも関わらず、Used、Fragmented、Freeの合計サイズが、10月の「SID_100」と11月の「SID_000」で同一です。 また、このサイズはS/4HANAのトランザクションコードDBACOCKPITから「現在のステータス」→「概要」を選択することで確認できる「データベースディスク使用率」の「ディスクサイズ」と一致しています。DBACOCKPITで確認できるこのサイズはクライアントごとではなく、ディスク全体のサイズを表している認識です。 EWAジョブはクライアントごとに実行されているとしても、ディスク使用率の情報収集はクライアントごとではなく、ディスク全体の情報を収集しているのではないでしょうか。 SAP for Me（ https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/dataoverview ） に表示される「HANA Disk Usage」は、クライアントごとではなく、ディスク全体の結果ではないでしょうか。

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration – Connecting to Messaging Systems using the AMQP Adapter
entry.content:
Hi @mandy_krimmel 

That last screenshot is from the Event Mesh UI Client, I´m inspecting the message directly in the broker.

The future consumer, is a BTP APP, so no iflow will consume that message. The idea is to create a Request/Reply Async pattern using the CorrelationID, so the BTP APP can place a new message as a response.

Anyways I´ll try to inspect the message with a different client (iflow + Allowed Headers // Node.js Client) to check if that EM UI Client might not be showing certain message properties.

Thank you very much for your response

Mikel

entry.title: Re: BSEG-EBELN and EBELP not populated at BTE 1120 structure
entry.content:
Some hints

  • Did you start from 2782220 - MIRO: Account assignment object is filled in FI Document from Invoice - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA 
  • Check Field Status Group associated to Account number, does it allow input of PO number and item number
  • Be aware the BTE wont affect FAGLFLEXA/ACDOC only BSEG
  • Are fields related to invoice set (REBZG, REBZJ, REBZZ)

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM: requirement approval with external workflow management system
entry.content:
Hi,

Today, custom approval status is not available. You can use the task API to add comments.

entry.title: Re: Outbound Delivery show error msg: VL063
entry.content:
Hello @0819 

Your requirement is unclear to me. The over-delivery tolerance will apply to the entire sales order quantity

For example,

The customer has placed an order for 10 units, with an over-delivery tolerance of 10%, divided into two schedule lines of 5 units each.

When creating the delivery for the first schedule line, you can deliver all units if sufficient stock is available. However, if you deliver only 7 units, the remaining 3 units for the second delivery will allow a maximum of 4 units to be delivered, considering the over-delivery tolerance

Conclusion : For the example above, you can deliver a maximum of 11 units.

Please apply same logic for you business requirement.

Please check and let us know

Best Regard

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: What You Need to Know About Blogging Post-Migration
entry.content: Hi all, I have a very general question: Is it only the author of a blog post who can edit a post after publication? Or is it also possible for someone else to make changes? Or would it in theory be possible to change the author of a published post to a different user? Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Create link between Handling Unit and Inbound Deliver...
entry.content:
Hello,

This is not yet possible (Hus for inbound deliveries are technically a bit different than “normal” Hus)

We have this feature on our list for new development. We hope this will be developed soon. But I cannot guarantee that this will be already available in the next main release.

Regards,

Ansgar

entry.title: Re: On-stack Clean Core Extensibility: Plain and Simple
entry.content: @JAEUK Thanks. I just had to clarify the bit about AEM because, in our situation, it was somehow perceived as something that can help you decide if something that is clean or not, which is not its purpose. For us, it was fortunately decided to go greenfield, which makes the journey to clean core considerably easier. Having to migrate all the legacy code from a 20-year old SAP system and trying to make it clean has a much lower chance of success, IMO.

entry.title: SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 Released – Safeguarding Customer Investments and Maximizing Value
entry.content:
With the general availability of SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 as of November 20, 2024, we’re introducing significant enhancements to improve scalability, reliability, user experience, and reduce the total cost of ownership. The SAP HANA database currently supports over 70,000 customers, powering some of the world’s most critical applications and workloads. Our customers, partners, and internal teams value SAP HANA for its stability, reliability, sustainability, ease of use, and cost-effectiveness. With SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08, we’ve focused on these priorities to protect our customers’ investments, while continuing to drive value in this leading translytical and multi-model database platform.

 

Broad Range of Innovative Features and Benefits

SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 introduces a range of valuable new features that make the in-memory computing environment more accessible and user-friendly. These enhancements empower more team members to obtain rapid insights and make data-driven decisions. Additional improvements focus on reducing the total cost of ownership, boosting scalability and data reliability, enhancing user experience, and more.

 

Here are just some of the many significant new features, capabilities, and enhancements you’ll find in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08:

  • Backup & Recovery now supports AES-256-CTR encryption for persistency, log and backup.
  • SAP HANA Native Storage Extension (NSE) now automatically applies recommendations and supports dynamic aging for range partitions, which automatically creates new range partitions.
  • Partitioning now includes three-level partitioning with Range-Range-Hash and Range-Range-Range type of partitions and comes with an enhanced multistep partitioning wizard in the SAP HANA Cockpit.
  • SAP HANA now restricts the connections between the user group members using IP / Network addresses, applications, and authentication method conditions.
  • X.509 certificates now supports additional wildcard values.
  • The SAP HANA Database Explorer is now available as an extension for Visual Studio Code.
  • Enhanced Machine Learning capabilities in multiple areas, like classification, time series, forecasting, and regression, to support the development of next-generation intelligent applications.
  • Updated SAP HANA Smart Data Access & Smart Data Integration  adapter certifications and new data provisioning capabilities, allowing for improved and seamless data federation and replication capabilities.
  • Modeling enhancements for column-based pruning and greedy pruning, along with the support of user-defined functions in expressions of calculated columns and filters, which provide better performance and more data modeling flexibility.
  • Updated XSA Runtimes for TomCat, Node.js, and SAPMachine provided for continued deployment flexibility.
  • Enhanced SAP HANA on-premises to SAP HANA Cloud migration assistant in SAP Build now supports XSC to XSA and CAP migrations.

 

Future Release & Maintenance Strategy

Starting with SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08, we’re planning to deliver new SAP HANA 2.0 versions every 2 years and always in Q4 of the respective calendar year. In addition, each SAP HANA 2.0 version will provide a 4-year maintenance period. This will provide a clear and predictable SAP HANA release and maintenance approach. In addition, customers will always have a 2-year overlap between two consecutive versions, offering sufficient time to plan and upgrade to the most recent version. 

 

Want to learn more about the new SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 release? If so, please register for the upcoming “What’s New in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 Overview Session” delivered by our SAP HANA Product Management team.

Check out the detailed What’s New Sessions that are scheduled on YouTube Live to learn more about the various enhancements.

If you have questions, please visit the SAP Community Q&A for SAP HANA or submit your question directly.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Platform Trial 1909 Available Now
entry.content:
@julieplummer20 What!? 5 years, you say? It cannot be! Where has the time gone? 😊

I am glad you still offer this trial. I think it is important to foster learning and innovation in the community to give developers a system to play with.

entry.title: Re: Meaningless "posted a topic/replied to topic", instead use "question", &quot
entry.content: Thank you, Sandra. Your feedback has been received. We will get back to you as soon as we have updates.

entry.title: Re: Can we create the new depreciation key in sap in SAP...
entry.content: My client has a requirement for the SLM Depreciation Key with a 5% cut-off. However, this Dep Key is not currently available in South Africa. Could you please advise on the process to obtain it.

entry.title: Re: FILTER Button in TABLE CONTROL
entry.content: Please help I got same requirment to add filter functionality in table control in mpp

entry.title: Re: FILTER Button in TABLE CONTROL
entry.content: Please send me the code As I got same requirment

entry.title: Perform SAP Principal Propagation with Microsoft Entra ID for SAP SuccessFactors!
entry.content:
This blog is co-authored with Vinayak Adkoli (Lead Product Manager, SAP Integration Suite, SAP SE). Link to Microsoft Learn Hub for Power Platform and SAP here🔗.

Dear community,

The last blog post showed how to integrate Microsoft and SAP low code solutions with SAP services using the API Management capability of SAP Integration Suite and Microsoft Entra ID as identity provider overall. Todays post is about the specifics for SAP SuccessFactors.

Spoiler alert🤫: the approach stays the same! Only a slightly modified API Management policy needs to be imported. SuccessFactors uses API keys for its SAML assertions rather than an OAuth2 client id and contains your SuccessFactors company id.

 

Technically you could use the same policy for NetWeaver based systems and SuccessFactors, because the “unexpected” authentication attributes are ignored as of the releases that were tested. It is a good practice though to separate the concerns. Therefore we published a separate policy instead of a one-size-fits all one with bloated number of attributes.

At the core of the solution is again the proven OAuth2SAMLBearer flow. See the official SuccessFactors docs on the required SAML attributes for reference.

In addition to that, solving this challenge on Integration Suite level enables scaling the approach to arbitrary many different consumer solutions. No more re-inventing the wheel for every developer!

Find the SuccessFactors specific policy on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Of course, you may deviate from the blueprint outlined based on your scenario across SAP BTP, SAP Graph, Integration Suite, other SAP SaaS solutions etc.

A glimpse under the hood

Start your journey on SAP API Management by creating a new API provider for your SuccessFactors tenant. Be aware☝️ of the different domains for the portal UI and the associated OData API endpoint! See SAP note 2215682 for reference.

 

Next, register your SuccessFactors OData API. In my sample I am taking the entity set “User”, that resides on the base path for a tenant in DC5 like so:

https://api5.successfactors.eu/odata/v2/User/$metadata

Make sure to verify the base path of the URL (/odata/v2 vs. /odata/v2/User) of your target endpoint on API Management. See below Screenshot for reference.

Otherwise, you will encounter routing issues for request like /odata/v2/User(‘1000’).

 

The API Management policy works under the assumption that trust between your SAP SuccessFactors OAuth2 server and Microsoft Entra ID has been setup before. See this guide for reference.

In addition to the existing authorizations maintained on SuccessFactors each application consuming the SAP API proxy from API Management need to be authorized on Entra ID.

 

See this official guide for details on the Entra ID setup. Be aware the linked guide refers to the SAML2 setup (not OAuth2!). See the difference for OAuth2SAMLBearer below:

 

The Entity ID can be any URI. It is a good practice though to use something that resembles your SuccessFactors tenant.

Be aware that Entity ID must be unique in your Entra ID tenant. In case you want to use SAML2 for SSO and OAuth2 for SAP Principal Propagation for this tenant at the same time, you need to maintain both on the Entra ID enterprise app registration. Assign an order (index) that works with your login flow.

 

Take care of your OAuth settings

The steps for the OAuth configuration may vary by SAP product. Here the focus is on SAP SuccessFactors.

Move on to your SuccessFactors tenant and open the app “Manage OAuth2 Client Applications” to create a new OAuth client for your Microsoft Entra ID enterprise app. Populate the values as per your environment.

Be aware that you need to copy the certificate body only without the header -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE----- and -----END CERTIFICATE-----.

 

Import the policy into your tenant

Download the policy from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and import the template into your SAP API Management tenant.

 

Apply to all the PostFlow steps of the TargetEndpoints of your APIs as you see fit. See this SAP tutorial and this SAP GitHub repos for more details.

 

Consider the governance and security trade-offs between mighty policies and SAP OAuth clients authorized for everything vs. a multitude of clients for each API and scope.

Configure the policy using a key value map

All the configuration needed for earlier shown token exchange flow is best provided with an encoded key value map (I recommend starting with un-encoded one, when you do this the first time for simpler troubleshooting). Create a new encrypted one called “SAPPrincipalPropagationMap”. The name is referenced on the provided policy.

 

Fill the values as per your environment:

Key

Value sample

Hints

entra-id-tenant-id

12a345bc-1234-56ab-78ab-zzzzzzzzz

Find it on the Azure portal (e.g. here)

issuer

https://sts.windows.net/12a345bc-1234-56ab-78ab-zzzzzzzzz/

The prefix is fixed. Only the tenant id is dynamic.

 

https://login.microsoftonline.com/12a345bc-1234-56ab-78ab-zzzzzzzzz for v2 endpoints

entra-id-audience

api://999abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

The globally unique Application ID URI from the Entra ID app registration representing your SAP API Management instance

AADRegisteredAppClientId

999abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

The Application (client) id of the Entra ID app registration representing your SAP API Management instance.

AADRegisteredAppClientSecret

 

The secret created for the application 999abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

AADSAPResource

https://my.successfactors.eu

The provider’s name from your SuccessFactors SAML setup.

sap-oauth-sf-api-key

ABCDE1234FGH6789

API Key generated by SuccessFactors for your OAuth2 application. Instructions here.

sap-oauth-company-id

SF123456

Your SuccessFactors Company id. Find here.

SAPOAuthServerAdress

ForTokenEndpoint

salesdemo.successfactors.eu

OAuth endpoint of your successfactors instance.

 Adjust the name of the API provider as per your setup in the policy elements “fetchSAPOAuthToken”, and “GetCSRFToken”.

The final mile of integration

Ok, all homework is done. Now we get to go outside and enjoy the “low code” sun with SAP SuccessFactors 🌞Create your SAP OData connection in Power Automate, choose the authentication type Microsoft Entra ID and paste the URI of the Entra ID app registration that represents your API Management.

Hints on troubleshooting

  • Before applying the APIM policy consider running the sequence of authentication calls locally (with “line of sight” to NetWeaver of course) using a REST client. See this Postman collection for reference.

 

  • Verify the produced Entra ID tokens attributes using a safe JWT validator (e.g. DevToys). Don’t share your sensitive tokens on some website for validation!
    • iss (Issuer): Should read something like https://sts.windows.net/12a3456-zzz... or https://login.microsoftonline.com/12a3456-zzz... for v2 endpoints.
    • aud (Audience): Something like “api://bbbbbb-cccc-dddd-dddd-eeeeeeee”
    • scp (Scope): “user_impersonation”

 

  • For the SAML2 assertion exercise the same approach but do base64 decode and XML pretty print. Notepad++ with MIME tools -> Base64 decode and XML Tools -> pretty print does the job locally just fine. Again, don’t paste sensitive info online! Verify the following claims from your assertion:
    • AudienceRestriction -> Audience: Should be a URL containing your app id, e.g. https://my.successfactors.eu
    • Claims: Name, email or whatever you have configured to be used to identify the named SAP backend user.

 

  • While doing integration tests with the API Management policy consider decoding the key value map or use a public one till you are confident with your setup to see immediately what config values were provided.

 

  • Verify that your key value map changes are being pulled.

 

  • Cached a faulty token? Disable the caching policy step "LookupSAPTokens" using the enabled attribute on the XML or consider adding an API to clear the cache by user using the "InvalidateCache" policy step. 

 

  • 405 Method Not allowed (SAP note 3386802 SAP API Management generates PUT method entries for OData v2 and PATCH entries for OData v4 services. Power Automate's "Update entity" step uses the PATCH approach. In case of conflict, either choose the "Create any type of OData request" step and configure PUT instead on Power Automate or adjust the swagger definition using "Edit in API Designer" on SAP API Management to add PATCH to cater for this.

Thoughts on production readiness

SAP Integration Suite is more than ready for prime time as the de-facto standard for SAP heavy integrations.

The OAuthSAML2Bearer flow is an ever green discussed in the community at length for years and fully supported by SAP for service to service Principal Propagation.

The involved Entra ID app registration client secret can be governed with Azure automation or SAP Build Apps Process Automation. See this Microsoft article and this GitHub repos for reference.

Applying the battle-proven API management policy from the API hub ensures a configuration driven approach and clear update paths.

PowerAutomate also supports Principal Propagation with SuccessFactors directly. However, that would be a point-to-point integration without the governance and security benefits of having an API Management solution in between.

The SAP and Microsoft low code eco system is a natural fit for productivity across business needs that involve M365 (Microsoft Graph, Teams, Outlook, SharePoint Online, etc.) and SAP.

In terms of governance, SAP offers extensive integration with the Microsoft ecosystem. See this co-authored blog about SAP API Management integrating Azure APIs and this one about Azure API Center to handle a multitude of gateways in a single place.

Final Words

That’s a wrap 🌯. Today you saw how to extend the learnings from the first blog to enable SAP SuccessFactors for Power Automate with Principal Propagation through Microsoft Entra ID. The approach maps Microsoft identities to SAP named users to retain its SAP authorizations. In addition to that you learnt that a provided SAP API Management policy performs the heavy lifting of the authentication flow.

App developers and low coders no longer need to deal with the complexity of the principal propagation and get added benefit of token caching, and CSRF handling out-of-the-box.

Get started from here.

#Kudos to Martin Raepple for the contribution of the tricky part of the token dance.

Cheers

@vinayak_adkoli and Martin

entry.title: Re: FILTER Button in TABLE CONTROL
entry.content: Please send me the sample code .

entry.title: Re: Why S/4HANA Extensibility Options are crucial.
entry.content:
Hi Robert,

this series of articles is an excellent and holistic summary of the topic. I think it captures the challenges and basically shows the essence of what we do at SAP, together with partners and customers. (Beyond providing the business solutions of course.)

Regards, Oliver

entry.title: Re: Cloud Foundry app stops on its own by provisioned_use...
entry.content:
This is documented in the BTP help portal:

"Applications are stopped at midnight (or some time later depending on server load) relative to the region in which you created your trial account. If you're working in a time zone that is far from the region where your trial account was created, then your applications may stop during business hours."
